title: DNA extraction protocol for the thraustochytrid Aurantiochytrium limacinum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ogf6","text":"This is a \"quick \u0026 dirty\" method for rapid genomic DNA extraction usable for polymerase chain reactions (PCR). Reliable for use with the thraustochytrid, Aurantiochytrium limacinum, but is suitable for other similar organisms as well, including yeast or microalgae.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":154,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"qwhff","text":"**10% Triton solution:**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"93aso","text":"- Triton-100: 10 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b9kz9","text":"- DDW: 90 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"g5irb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vjjon","text":"**1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8):**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ztjop","text":"- Tris-HCl: 12.61 g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0xrj1","text":"- DDW: 80 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q11js","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ypi2d","text":"**0.5 M EDTA:**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sazdw","text":"- EDTA: 11.69 g","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4sbk","text":"- DDW: 80 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fuyou","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kuk4k","text":"**DNA extraction buffer:**","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bim3q","text":"- 10% Triton: 5 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhbth","text":"- 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8): 1 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ajfgn","text":"- 0.5 M EDTA: 200 µL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Colonies should be grown on an agar-media plate for 4 days, and no older than 2 months.
Add 20 µL of DNA Extraction Buffer to a PCR tube.
Scrape some colony using a plastic pipette tip.
Resuspend the colony in the 20 µL DNA Extraction Buffer.

Immediately remove PCR tube from thermocycler, and allow to cool to room temperature on the benchtop. [Room temperature cooling is important for several reasons. It allows for re-annealing of DNA to the more stable dsDNA form, as well as the precipitation of cellular debris.]
Once cooled to room temperature, add 80 µL of sterile nuclease-free deionized water, and mix by gently pipetting up and down.
Sample is now ready for use. 1-2 µL should be sufficient for a 50 µL PCR reaction. If not using, freeze at -20°C. For temporary storage, keep on ice, or in ice water, or at 4°C.
creator: Ben Leyland
